project_extract.article:
The «Data extraction» section controls how document content is obtained.

A key difficulty with many formats is that it is unclear what is on images: it may be a text scan, a photo, or a diagram.

Most settings relate to image handling; if you prefer not to tune everything, the defaults work for many cases.

Things to watch:
- Render scale and image size strongly affect cost and recognition time; values much above ~1500 px are rarely worth it
- If your PDFs are scans, they often have both an image layer and a low-quality OCR text layer; adaptive mode or full-page render is usually better
- If office documents truly have no images, disable image handling to save a bit on processing
project_extract.provider.markdown_extrac: Save as is — no changes. Save without YAML — strip front matter. Rebuild via LLM — prepare text for later generation (YAML removed).
project_extract.provider.pdf_extract_pro: «Adaptive» renders pages to images when large images or poor OCR text are detected. «Render all pages» sends every page as images
Page render scale for text recognition; higher values improve quality but increase processing cost
project_extract.provider.office_extract_: Works directly with document text; behavior for images depends on the mode. «Adaptive» skips tiny images such as logos
What to do with embedded images. If you are sure there are no scans or screenshots, choose «Ignore»
Images larger than this are scaled down proportionally. Larger images improve recognition quality but cost more
project_extract.provider.text_extract_pr: Simple loader: when enabled, text files are passed to the LLM unless extraction is turned off
project_extract.provider.image_extract_p: When image models are configured, the app tries to read text from images. Vector images (SVG) are rasterized using the size limit below
Images larger than this are scaled down proportionally. Larger images improve recognition quality but cost more
